subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Solve one-step equations using the Balancing Method
Understand the Transposition method for solving equations

lesson_plan.title: The Balancing Method
topics_covered: Adding/Subtracting the same number from both sides
Multiplying/Dividing both sides by the same non-zero number
teaching_strategy: Visualizing operations on a balance scale
[Image showing balancing an equation by adding or subtracting from both sides]
student_activities: Solve $x - 8 = 10$ by adding 8 to both sides
Solve $5y = 35$ by dividing both sides by 5
assessment: Board work showing all balancing steps

lesson_plan.title: The Transposition Method
teaching_strategy: The 'Bridge' analogy: Crossing the '=' bridge flips the sign
Flowchart of sign changes ($+ 	o -$, $	imes 	o \div$)
student_activities: Verify the answer by putting the value back into the original equation
assessment: Exit slip: 'Check if $x=4$ is the solution for $2x + 2 = 10$'

teacher_tip: Always tell students to 'Check your work!' by plugging the answer back in. It’s the only math topic where you can know if you got 100% before the teacher grades it.
